0.original: When is a person too young?
0.b1: At what age is someone considered too young?
0.cn: 一个人在什么年纪才算太年轻？
0.ai_notes: 'too young': used to describe someone who lacks the necessary age or maturity for a specific task or situation.
0.remarks: 'too young': 形容某人因年龄太小或不够成熟，无法胜任某项任务或应对某种情况。

1.original: The I'm-too-young variety of age excusitis does much damage, too.
1.b1: Using 'I am too young' as an excuse also causes many problems.
1.cn: 以“我太年轻了”为借口的这种心态，也会造成很大的负面影响。
1.ai_notes: 'excusitis': a humorous, made-up word combining 'excuse' and '-itis' (a suffix for diseases) to describe the habit of constantly making excuses. 'do much damage': to cause significant harm or negative consequences.
1.remarks:
'excusitis': 一个幽默的自造词，由“借口”和表示疾病的后缀“-itis”组成，用来形容经常找借口的习惯。
'do much damage': 造成严重的伤害或负面后果。

2.original: About a year ago, a twenty-three-year-old fellow named Jerry came to me with a problem.
2.b1: About a year ago, a 23-year-old man named Jerry came to me with a problem.
2.cn: 大约一年前，一个叫杰瑞的23岁小伙子带着一个问题来找我。
2.ai_notes: 'fellow': a friendly or informal way to refer to a man. 'came to me with a problem': a common way to say someone sought advice or help for an issue.
2.remarks:
'fellow': 对男性的友好或非正式称呼。
'came to me with a problem': 寻求建议或帮助解决问题的常用说法。

3.original: Jerry was a fine young man.
3.b1: Jerry was a very good young man.
3.cn: 杰瑞是个很棒的年轻人。
3.ai_notes: 'fine': in this context, it means high-quality, capable, or admirable.
3.remarks: 'fine': 在此语境下，指高质量的、有能力的或令人赞赏的。

4.original: He had been a paratrooper in the service and then had gone to college.
4.b1: He had served as a paratrooper and then went to college.
4.cn: 他曾是一名伞兵，退伍后上了大学。
4.ai_notes: 'paratrooper': a soldier who is trained to jump out of airplanes using a parachute. 'in the service': a common way to refer to serving in the military.
4.remarks:
'paratrooper': 受过训练、使用降落伞从飞机上跳下的士兵。
'in the service': 指在军队服役的常用说法。

5.original: While going to college, Jerry supported his wife and son by selling for a large transfer and storage company.
5.b1: While in college, Jerry supported his family by working for a large moving and storage company.
5.cn: 上大学期间，杰瑞通过在一家大型搬运仓储公司工作来养活妻子和儿子。
5.ai_notes: 'supported his wife and son': to provide the money needed for food, housing, and other living costs for one's family. 'transfer and storage company': a business that helps people move their belongings to new homes.
5.remarks:
'supported his wife and son': 为家人提供食物、住房及其他生活开支所需的金钱。
'transfer and storage company': 帮助人们将物品搬运到新居的搬家仓储公司。

6.original: He had done a terrific job, both in college and for his company.
6.b1: He performed very well both at college and at his job.
6.cn: 他在大学和工作中都表现得非常出色。
6.ai_notes: 'terrific': an adjective meaning excellent or very good.
6.remarks: 'terrific': 形容词，意为极好的或非常棒的。

7.ai_notes: 'worried': feeling anxious or troubled about actual or potential problems.
7.remarks: 'worried': 对现实或潜在的问题感到焦虑或不安。

8.original: Dr. Schwartz, he said, I've got a problem.
8.b1: He said, 'Dr. Schwartz, I have a problem.'
8.cn: 他说：“施瓦茨博士，我遇到了一个问题。”
8.ai_notes: 'I've got a problem': a standard, informal way to state that one is facing a difficulty.
8.remarks: 'I've got a problem': 表示自己正面临困难的标准非正式说法。

9.original: My company has offered me the job of sales manager.
9.b1: My company offered me a job as a sales manager.
9.cn: 公司提拔我担任销售经理。
9.ai_notes: 'offered me the job': when an employer asks someone to take a specific position.
9.remarks: 'offered me the job': 雇主邀请某人担任特定职位。

10.original: This would make me supervisor over eight salesmen.
10.b1: This means I will be the boss of eight other salesmen.
10.cn: 这意味着我将成为八名销售员的上司。
10.ai_notes: 'supervisor': a person who manages or oversees the work of others. 'over': in this context, it means having authority or control 'above' someone else.
10.remarks:
'supervisor': 管理或监督他人工作的人。
'over': 在此语境下，指对某人拥有权威或控制权。

11.original: Congratulations, that's wonderful news, I said.
11.b1: I said, 'Congratulations, that is great news.'
11.cn: 我说：“恭喜你，这是个好消息。”
11.ai_notes: 'wonderful news': an expression used to show excitement or happiness for someone else's success.
11.remarks: 'wonderful news': 用来表达对他人的成功感到兴奋或高兴的短语。

12.ai_notes: 'seem': to appear to be a certain way based on observation.
12.remarks: 'seem': 根据观察，看起来是某种样子。

13.original: Well, he continued, all eight men I'm to supervise are from seven to 21 years older than I. What do you think I should do?
13.b1: He said, 'All eight men I will manage are 7 to 21 years older than me. What should I do?'
13.cn: 他接着说：“我要管理的八个人都比我大7到21岁。你觉得我该怎么办？”
13.ai_notes: 'supervise': to be in charge of people and ensure they do their work correctly. 'What do you think I should do?': a common way to ask for advice.
13.remarks:
'supervise': 负责监督他人，确保他们正确完成工作。
'What do you think I should do?': 寻求建议的常用方式。

14.original: Can I handle it?" Jerry, I said, the general manager of your company obviously thinks you're old enough or he wouldn't have offered you this job.
14.b1: 'Can I do this?' I said, 'Jerry, your manager clearly thinks you are old enough, or he would not have offered you the job.'
14.cn: “我能胜任吗？”我说，“杰瑞，你们经理显然认为你足够成熟了，否则他不会给你这份工作。”
14.ai_notes: 'handle it': to be able to manage or deal with a difficult situation. 'obviously': clearly or easily understood.
14.remarks:
'handle it': 有能力处理或应对困难情况。
'obviously': 清楚地或容易理解地。

15.original: Just remember these three points and everything will work out just fine.
15.b1: Just remember these three points and everything will be fine.
15.cn: 只要记住这三点，一切都会好起来的。
15.ai_notes: 'work out': to result in a successful or positive outcome.
15.remarks: 'work out': 产生成功或积极的结果。

16.b1: First, do not worry about your age.
16.cn: 首先，不要太在意自己的年龄。
16.ai_notes: 'age-conscious': being overly aware or worried about one's own age in relation to others.
16.remarks: 'age-conscious': 过分在意或担心自己相对于他人的年龄。

17.original: Back on the farm, a boy became a man when he proved he could do the work of a man.
17.b1: In the past, a boy became a man when he proved he could do a man's work.
17.cn: 过去在农场里，当一个男孩证明他能承担成年人的工作时，他就是一个男人了。
17.ai_notes: 'Back on the farm': an idiom used to refer to a simpler time or a place where people learn practical life lessons through hard work.
17.remarks: 'Back on the farm': 一个习语，指代一段更简单的时光，或指通过辛勤劳动学习生活实践经验的地方。

18.original: His number of birthdays had nothing to do with it, and this applies to you.
18.b1: Your age does not matter, and this is true for you too.
18.cn: 你的年龄并不重要，这对你也适用。
18.ai_notes: 'number of birthdays': a way to refer to a person's chronological age. 'applies to you': means that a rule or situation is also true for you.
18.remarks:
'number of birthdays': 指代一个人的实际年龄。
'applies to you': 指某条规则或情况同样适用于你。

19.original: When you prove you are able to handle the job of sales manager, you're automatically old enough.
19.b1: When you show that you can do the job of a sales manager, you are old enough.
19.cn: 当你证明自己能胜任销售经理的工作时，你就已经足够成熟了。
19.ai_notes: 'automatically': happening as a natural result without needing extra effort or thought.
19.remarks: 'automatically': 作为自然结果发生，无需额外努力或思考。

20.original: Second, don't take advantage of your new gold bars.
20.b1: Second, do not misuse your new authority.
20.cn: 第二，不要滥用你的新职权。
20.ai_notes: 'take advantage of': to use something for one's own benefit, often in an unfair way. 'gold bars': a metaphor for the symbols of rank or authority (like military badges).
20.remarks:
'take advantage of': 利用某事来谋取私利，通常指不公平的方式。
'gold bars': 比喻职级或权力的象征（如军衔徽章）。

21.ai_notes: 'show respect': to act in a way that demonstrates you value someone else's experience or position.
21.remarks: 'show respect': 通过行为表现出你对他人的经验或职位的重视。

22.ai_notes: 'suggestions': ideas or plans offered for consideration.
22.remarks: 'suggestions': 提供给他人考虑的想法或计划。

23.original: Make them feel they are working for a team captain, not a dictator.
23.b1: Make them feel that you are a team leader, not a boss who gives orders.
23.cn: 让他们觉得你是一位团队领袖，而不是一个发号施令的独裁者。
23.ai_notes: 'team captain': a leader who works alongside the team. 'dictator': a leader who forces others to obey without listening to them.
23.remarks:
'team captain': 与团队并肩工作的领导者。
'dictator': 强迫他人服从而不听取意见的领导者。

24.original: Do this and the men will work with you, not against you.
24.b1: If you do this, the men will work with you, not against you.
24.cn: 这样做，大家就会配合你，而不是抵触你。
24.ai_notes: 'work with you, not against you': a phrase meaning to cooperate rather than resist or cause trouble.
24.remarks: 'work with you, not against you': 指合作而不是抵触或制造麻烦。

25.original: Third, get used to having older persons working for you.
25.b1: Third, get used to having older people working for you.
25.cn: 第三，习惯让比你年长的人为你工作。
25.ai_notes: 'get used to': to become comfortable with a situation that was previously strange or difficult.
25.remarks: 'get used to': 对以前感到陌生或困难的情况变得习惯。

26.original: Leaders in all fields soon find they are younger than many of the people they supervise.
26.b1: Leaders often find they are younger than many of the people they manage.
26.cn: 各行各业的领导者很快就会发现，他们往往比许多下属更年轻。
26.ai_notes: 'in all fields': in every area of work or industry.
26.remarks: 'in all fields': 在每一个工作或行业领域。

27.original: So get used to having older men work for you.
27.ai_notes: 'get used to it': a common phrase meaning to accept a new reality because it is not going to change.
27.remarks: 'get used to it': 一个常用短语，意为接受既定事实，因为情况不会改变。

28.original: It will help you a lot in the coming years, when even bigger opportunities develop.
28.b1: It will help you a lot in the future when you have even bigger opportunities.
28.cn: 这会对你未来的职业发展有很大帮助，尤其是在面对更大的机会时。
28.ai_notes: 'coming years': the future. 'develop': to grow or happen over time.
28.remarks:
'coming years': 未来。
'develop': 随时间推移而成长或发生。

29.original: And remember, Jerry, your age won't be a handicap unless you make it one.
29.b1: And remember, Jerry, your age is only a problem if you think it is.
29.cn: 记住，杰瑞，除非你自己觉得年龄是个问题，否则它就不是障碍。
29.ai_notes: 'handicap': an obstacle or a disadvantage that makes it harder to succeed.
29.remarks: 'handicap': 使成功变得更困难的障碍或劣势。

30.b1: Today, Jerry is doing very well.
30.ai_notes: 'doing fine': a common way to say someone is performing well or is in a good situation.
30.remarks: 'doing fine': 形容某人表现良好或处于良好状态的常用说法。

31.original: He loves the transportation business, and now he's planning to organize his own company in a few years.
31.b1: He loves the business and plans to start his own company in a few years.
31.cn: 他热爱运输行业，并计划在几年内创办自己的公司。
31.ai_notes: 'transportation business': the industry of moving goods or people from one place to another.
31.remarks: 'transportation business': 运输货物或人员的行业。

32.original: Youth is a liability only when the youth thinks it is.
32.b1: Being young is only a disadvantage if you believe it is.
32.cn: 年轻只有在你自己认为它是劣势时，才会成为劣势。
32.ai_notes: 'liability': a disadvantage or something that causes problems. 'only when... thinks it is': emphasizes that one's mindset determines whether a trait is a strength or a weakness.
32.remarks:
'liability': 一种劣势或导致问题的因素。
'only when... thinks it is': 强调个人的思维方式决定了某种特质是优势还是劣势。
